threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the tube' instead of 'the underground'. Watch out, the locals will know you’re fresh off the boat!
Last night on the 59, a guy serenaded the bus with 'Wonderwall'—and somehow, everyone joined in. Brixton really knows how to lift a Monday!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I stumbled upon a spontaneous dance party in the courtyard, complete with a DJ and fairy lights. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a currywurst stand next to a hipster coffee shop, where you can order a flat white at 3 AM after a night out.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they complain about the BART delays but still consider taking a bus under any circumstance. Never happens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni driver singing along to Sinatra while stuck in traffic near 16th. Only in SF do delays come with a soundtrack.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think plazas are for sunbathing, but locals use them for evening chats over vermut, keeping the vibe alive long after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they still clutch a map, even when lost in the metros. The locals just glide through without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the transport' and stare blankly when someone asks if you prefer the Overground.
Caught a late 35 bus near Brixton and an old lady started singing 'My Way' like it was a concert. Entire bus was her audience. Proper vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a guy bartering for a falafel while balancing a toddler on his shoulders during a bus delay. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find a Späti with a mini art gallery inside. Only in Berlin would a convenience store proudly showcase local artists' work.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they speed-walk past a BART delay with a coffee, while newcomers just stand there, bewildered.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo and flip-flops juggling oranges on the bus. The driver just sighed and rolled his eyes. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the bars close up tight at midnight, but you'll find locals sipping vermut well into the early hours, especially on weekends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still squint at the metro maps like they're trying to decode an ancient scroll.
